synopsis: The biography follows Claude Shannon from a curious, mechanically inventive childhood through his work in cryptography, electrical engineering, and the mathematical theory of information. It explains how his ideas underpin digital communication and computing while also tracing the puzzles, machines, gambling experiments, and playful habits that shape his approach to discovery.

sources.title: A Mind at Play eBook | Official Publisher Page | Simon & Schuster

synopsis: The book organizes more than 250 recurring design problems into linked patterns for shaping towns, neighborhoods, buildings, rooms, and construction details. Each pattern states a problem, explains the forces that make it difficult, and proposes a solution, allowing residents and designers to combine patterns into coherent environments suited to particular places and needs.

sources.title: A Pattern Language | Oxford University Press
A pattern language: towns, buildings, construction | WorldCat.org

synopsis: The book treats complexity as the central obstacle in software design and presents principles for decomposing systems into modules that remain understandable and easy to change. It examines deep interfaces, information hiding, dependency management, general-purpose design, error handling, comments, naming, and strategic investment in design, using examples to show how small choices accumulate into maintainable or fragile code.

sources.title: Discussion of A Philosophy of Software Design
A Philosophy of Software Design | Open Library

synopsis: The book traces the postwar transformation of middle-class Americans from cautious savers into active borrowers and investors as credit cards, mutual funds, retirement accounts, and discount brokerages spread. It follows the financial innovators and economic forces behind these products while examining how easier access to markets changes household habits, personal responsibility, risk, and attitudes toward money.

sources.title: A Piece of the Action | Official Publisher Page | Simon & Schuster
A Piece of the Action | Google Books

synopsis: The book introduces electronics through practical explanations and experiments, beginning with amplification and transistors before progressing through feedback, impedance matching, semiconductor behavior, power supplies, pulse handling, and analogue integrated circuits. Later chapters cover oscillators, digital logic, microcomputer circuits, component identification, device selection, operational-amplifier data, and computer interfacing while keeping mathematical treatment subordinate to circuit concepts.

sources.title: A Practical Introduction to Electronic Circuits | Cambridge University Press
A Practical Introduction to Electronic Circuits, Third Edition: Table of Contents

synopsis: The textbook develops measure-theoretic probability for graduate students in statistics, engineering, biology, finance, and other applied fields, building the necessary measure theory within the probability course itself. It moves through random variables, integration, expectation, independence, modes of convergence, laws of large numbers, central limit results, conditional expectation, and martingales, with a one-semester route through the material.

sources.title: A Probability Path | Springer Nature Link

synopsis: The memoir follows Obama's path from early political aspirations and community organizing through the 2008 election and the opening years of his presidency. It recounts forming an administration and confronting the financial crisis, health-care reform, Afghanistan strategy, Wall Street regulation, the Deepwater Horizon disaster, and the operation against Osama bin Laden, while reflecting on presidential power, partisan politics, diplomacy, race, and family life.

sources.title: A Promised Land | Penguin Random House

synopsis: Centuries after Panga’s self-aware robots left human society for the wilderness, a wandering tea monk encounters a robot tasked with fulfilling an old promise to check on humanity. The pair travel together and repeatedly confront the robot’s question of what people need, exploring purpose, sufficiency, consciousness, and humanity’s relationship with the natural world.

sources.title: A Psalm for the Wild-Built | Tor Publishing Group

synopsis: In a near-future California overwhelmed by the addictive Substance D, undercover narcotics agent Bob Arctor is ordered to surveil a household of drug users, including himself. As the drug divides his brain and erodes his ability to distinguish Arctor from his police persona, the investigation turns into a crisis of identity, loyalty, perception, and institutional control.

sources.title: A Scanner Darkly | Penguin Random House
